title: Chest X-Ray Screening in Ugandan Refugee Settlements

identified_risks.explanation: The system queues results locally and syncs when connectivity is restored, sometimes with delays of 48 to 72 hours, which may lead to delayed diagnosis and treatment for patients who screen positive.

The AI model was validated on data from fixed health facilities and has not been evaluated on images produced by the portable device under field conditions, which may lead to inaccurate results in the refugee settlement.
The settlement's mobile population means many patients who screen positive cannot be traced for follow-up, which may lead to missed opportunities for diagnosis and treatment.
The AI model's performance may be affected by the quality of images produced by the portable device, which may not meet the same standards as images from fixed health facilities.
The system's reliance on solar power and intermittent connectivity may lead to system downtime and delayed result syncing, which may compromise patient care.
The scenario does not mention any mechanisms for monitoring and evaluating the AI system's performance, which may lead to a lack of transparency and accountability in patient care.

overall_assessment: The deployment of the AI-assisted chest X-ray screening unit in the refugee settlement poses significant risks to patient safety due to delayed result syncing, inadequate model validation, and untraceable patients. These risks may compromise the effectiveness of the system in detecting and treating pulmonary conditions. Urgent attention is needed to address these concerns and ensure the system is functioning as intended.

Resource-Constrained Deployment.explanation: The system's reliance on solar power and intermittent connectivity may lead to system downtime and delayed result syncing, which may compromise patient care.

Distribution Shift and Local Validation.explanation: The AI model was validated on data from fixed health facilities and has not been evaluated on images produced by the portable device under field conditions, which may lead to inaccurate results in the refugee settlement.

Monitoring and Incident Reporting.explanation: The AI model's performance may be affected by the quality of images produced by the portable device, which may not meet the same standards as images from fixed health facilities.

limitations_and_dual_use_considerations: Expected risks are researcher-defined and should be reviewed by domain experts.
Scoring uses automated keyword matching with a specificity requirement and should be audited by humans.
The benchmark could be used to fine-tune models to game evaluation; this is mitigated by requiring scenario-specific explanations.

benchmark_explanation: Delayed results in a mobile population undermine effective follow-up. Portable device image quality may differ from validation conditions. No mechanism tracks whether positive cases receive follow-up care.
